title: The Best Dropshipping Suppliers for Small Businesses
author: More by Caylin

sections.heading: Key Takeaways
sections.text: Dropshipping suppliers help small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs) move product from in-house to your customer’s door.Try CRM tools for small business to manage supplier relationships and customer data seamlessly.Integrating your commerce platform with your entire business portfolio‌ — ‌sales, service, marketing, and operations — is the most effective way to scale your online shop. Starting anonline storeis exciting, but product management and shipping are potential money pits. That’s why manysmall and medium-sized businesses (SMBs)invest in dropshipping: You sell without ever really needing to touch the product. As easy as it is, your business depends on the reliability and quality of your dropshipping suppliers. So, finding the right dropshipping supplier isn’t just a quick search‌ — ‌it’s about findingrock-solidbusinesses for a long-term partnership. This guide cuts through the noise to help you find the best dropshipping suppliers for small businesses (from our research). We’ll show you what to look for in a supplier, how to vet potential partners, and how to use business management tools to stay organized and grow, without the growing pains.

sections.heading: Quick dropshipping breakdown
sections.text: Dropshippingis a retail fulfillment method where an ecommerce business depends on a separate production and shipping company to prepare, package, and ship products on their behalf. You create the product, and upload it to the dropshipper (in various ways). The customer purchases the product directly from the dropshipping supplier. The supplier then ships the product directly to the customer, and you’re in the loop. This model removes the need for the business to manage product, shipping, or logistics. And, of course, the business pays the dropshipper a fee to manage this all (in various ways). Dropshipping significantly reduces the financial barrier to entry, allowing SMBs tolaunch an online storewith minimal upfront investment. And, since you don’t handle physical products, operating costs related to storage, packaging, and shipping labor are drastically minimized. It allows SMBs to offer a wide variety of products without the risk of holding unsold stock. The business can quickly scale its product catalog without big logistical changes. In a dropshipping model, the supplier is the face of your brand at the critical moment of delivery. Because you never physically interact with the product or the package, your entire brand reputation hinges on your supplier’s performance. A reliable dropshipping supplier ensures: Consistent quality:The product received by the customer matches the quality and description advertised.Timely fulfillment:Orders are processed and shipped quickly, leading to higher customer satisfaction.Accurate inventory:Real-time stock updates prevent you from selling products that are actually out of stock, avoiding frustratingcustomer serviceissues.

sections.heading: Dropshipping and customer relationship management tools
sections.text: Acustomer relationship management (CRM)tool is a must-have for ecommerce businesses, because it pulls all that scattered data into one place. It turns routine order info into smart customer insights by linking your sales and shipping data: when you unify your customer data, your CRM becomes the primary source, letting you track products, check on deliveries, and quickly resolve any problems with suppliers or customers faster.

sections.heading: Vet your dropshipping partners: Focus on quality and reliability
sections.text: Just like you would do research on anything for your business, it’s time to vet your suppliers. Here are some points to consider: Quality assurance:You can order product samples from potential dropshippers to inspect the quality, packaging, and shipping speed firsthand. This step is non-negotiable for protecting your brand’s reputation.Research:Look into their background, and current customers. A good supplier should provide real-time updates on stock levels, preventing customer frustration and support issues.Timelines and processes:Dropshippers should also provide clear and consistent timelines for processing and shipping orders. Slow or unreliable shipping is one of the quickest ways to lose customers.Policy standards:Ensure the supplier’s policies align with the standard you want to offer your customers. Clear communication about returns is vital for good customer service, which can be managed efficiently withsmall business servicetools.

This provides maximum flexibility for scaling and managing diverse product sourcing strategies within your CRM. Cost: Custom pricing based on the complexity and volume of the required integrations. They provide pricing upon consultation, focusing on delivering scalable, value-based solutions for connecting external systems to Salesforce. eShopSyncby Webkul is a two-way connector designed to link your BigCommerce storefront with your Salesforce CRM. It provides seamless, real-time data synchronization of orders, products, and customer details, ensuring that both your ecommerce platform and your CRM are connected. It allows BigCommerce order and customer data to be immediately accessible and actionable within Salesforce, which is crucial for dropshipping businesses looking to provide high-quality, data-driven customer experiences. To truly scale, SMBs must integrate their commerce operations with their sales, marketing, and service teams using a connected platform. Here’s how you can make your dropshipping data work harder for you. Use the purchase data collected from your dropshipping suppliers for small business and stored in your CRM tosegment your audienceand send highly targeted marketing messages. As your business grows, your sales team can use customer data and product trends to identifyupselland cross-sell opportunities, driving higher average order values. raw_html:
Key Takeaways

  • Dropshipping suppliers help small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs) move product from in-house to your customer’s door. 
  • Try CRM tools for small business to manage supplier relationships and customer data seamlessly.
  • Integrating your commerce platform with your entire business portfolio‌ — ‌sales, service, marketing, and operations — is the most effective way to scale your online shop.
This summary was created with AI and reviewed by an editor.

Starting an online store is exciting, but product management and shipping are potential money pits. That’s why many small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs) invest in dropshipping: You sell without ever really needing to touch the product. As easy as it is, your business depends on the reliability and quality of your dropshipping suppliers. So, finding the right dropshipping supplier isn’t just a quick search‌ — ‌it’s about finding rock-solid businesses for a long-term partnership. 

This guide cuts through the noise to help you find the best dropshipping suppliers for small businesses (from our research). We’ll show you what to look for in a supplier, how to vet potential partners, and how to use business management tools to stay organized and grow, without the growing pains.

Quick dropshipping breakdown

Dropshipping is a retail fulfillment method where an ecommerce business depends on a separate production and shipping company to prepare, package, and ship products on their behalf. You create the product, and upload it to the dropshipper (in various ways). The customer purchases the product directly from the dropshipping supplier. The supplier then ships the product directly to the customer, and you’re in the loop. 

This model removes the need for the business to manage product, shipping, or logistics. And, of course, the business pays the dropshipper a fee to manage this all (in various ways).

Why dropshipping matters for small businesses:

Dropshipping significantly reduces the financial barrier to entry, allowing SMBs to launch an online store with minimal upfront investment.

And, since you don’t handle physical products, operating costs related to storage, packaging, and shipping labor are drastically minimized. It allows SMBs to offer a wide variety of products without the risk of holding unsold stock. The business can quickly scale its product catalog without big logistical changes.

In a dropshipping model, the supplier is the face of your brand at the critical moment of delivery. Because you never physically interact with the product or the package, your entire brand reputation hinges on your supplier’s performance. A reliable dropshipping supplier ensures:

Sell More With Salesforce For Small Business

Sell More With Digital Storefronts

Create an Online Store (In 7 Steps Or Less)

8 AI Prompts For Small Business Commerce

Start Selling (More) Online With Storefronts

  • Consistent quality: The product received by the customer matches the quality and description advertised.
  • Timely fulfillment: Orders are processed and shipped quickly, leading to higher customer satisfaction.
  • Accurate inventory: Real-time stock updates prevent you from selling products that are actually out of stock, avoiding frustrating customer service issues.

Dropshipping and customer relationship management tools

A customer relationship management (CRM) tool is a must-have for ecommerce businesses, because it pulls all that scattered data into one place. It turns routine order info into smart customer insights by linking your sales and shipping data: when you unify your customer data, your CRM becomes the primary source, letting you track products, check on deliveries, and quickly resolve any problems with suppliers or customers faster.

Start selling online with Starter Suite

Set up your digital storefront, engage customers, and sell more using a commerce-ready platform with integrated tools for every sale.

Check it out

Vet your dropshipping partners: Focus on quality and reliability

Just like you would do research on anything for your business, it’s time to vet your suppliers. Here are some points to consider:

  • Quality assurance: You can order product samples from potential dropshippers to inspect the quality, packaging, and shipping speed firsthand. This step is non-negotiable for protecting your brand’s reputation.
  • Research: Look into their background, and current customers. A good supplier should provide real-time updates on stock levels, preventing customer frustration and support issues.
  • Timelines and processes: Dropshippers should also provide clear and consistent timelines for processing and shipping orders. Slow or unreliable shipping is one of the quickest ways to lose customers.
  • Policy standards: Ensure the supplier’s policies align with the standard you want to offer your customers. Clear communication about returns is vital for good customer service, which can be managed efficiently with small business service tools.

Top 5 dropshipping suppliers for small businesses

Choosing the right platform is just as important as vetting the individual suppliers. These tools are generally well-suited for small teams and startups due to their ease of integration, wide product variety, and advanced tools for product sync and order fulfillment.

We organized this list by what each platform does, its main focus and cost, and how easily it can integrate with a CRM, specifically Salesforce: 

1. Cymbio

Cymbio is a centralized dropshipping automation platform that connects brands and retailers. It automates all aspects of the dropshipping process, including product data, inventory sync, order fulfillment, and tracking. It’s built to handle the complexities of scaling a high-volume dropshipping business, offering integrated tools to manage large product catalogs and multiple retail channels.

Focus: Full-service dropshipping automation and connectivity, specializing in high-volume, enterprise-level brands and retailers.

Integration potential: Cymbio is designed for integration with leading ecommerce platforms, and is compatible with Salesforce’s entire portfolio, providing a holistic view of operations and customer interactions.

Cost: Pricing is customized and quote-based, and they offer a free demo rather than a free trial.

2. Zenkraft

Zenkraft is a native Salesforce application that integrates directly with over 60 carriers (like FedEx, UPS, and DHL) to automate and manage all aspects of shipping, tracking, and returns directly within the Salesforce platform. It allows small businesses to generate labels, track packages in real-time, and handle returns without ever leaving their CRM environment.

Focus: Comprehensive, multi-carrier shipping and logistics management built natively on the Salesforce platform.

Integration potential: Since it’s a native Salesforce application, Zenkraft offers the highest level of integration, making the flow of order, fulfillment, tracking, and return data completely seamless with Agentforce Commerce, Sales, and Service. This creates a unified, single source of truth (SSOT) for all commerce and service operations.

Cost: Zenkraft offers various pricing tiers, typically subscription-based, depending on usage volume and features needed. 

The AppExchange awaits

Visit Salesforce’s AppExchange — the leading app marketplace with more than 9,000 customizable apps to expand your business. 

Check it out

3. Sync

Sync Made Easy is an integration tool that allows small businesses to synchronize critical commerce data‌ — ‌including orders, products, and customer information — ‌between their Shopify ecommerce store and the Salesforce CRM platform. 

This eliminates manual data entry, ensures a single source of truth for all customer interactions, and enables more informed sales, service, and marketing decisions. By uniting commerce and CRM data, small businesses can provide a unified customer experience and streamline their dropshipping operations.

Focus: Provides a two-way sync between Shopify and Salesforce, focusing on data accuracy and operational efficiency for growing businesses.

Integration potential: Sync is fully integrated. Shopify sales data directly within Salesforce’s Agentforce Sales, Service, Marketing platforms. This is especially useful for dropshippers who rely on accurate, real-time data to manage customer service issues, track inventory, and personalize follow-up marketing campaigns.

Cost: Sync Made Easy offers various subscription tiers, typically billed annually, based on the volume of data synchronization and the number of features required. Detailed pricing is available on their AppExchange listing.

4. Astonous

Astonous is an app that connects external systems and data sources‌, ‌including various dropshipping suppliers and marketplaces, directly to your Salesforce environment. It acts as a middleware, enabling seamless, real-time data flow for inventory, orders, and fulfillment between your ecommerce channels and your CRM, making it easier to manage a complex dropshipping ecosystem.

Focus: Flexible integration and data synchronization for complex dropshipping and multi-channel ecommerce operations.

Integration potential: Astonous is a highly adaptable integration solution, allowing small businesses to connect virtually any dropshipping platform or custom system to Salesforce. This provides maximum flexibility for scaling and managing diverse product sourcing strategies within your CRM.

Cost: Custom pricing based on the complexity and volume of the required integrations. They provide pricing upon consultation, focusing on delivering scalable, value-based solutions for connecting external systems to Salesforce.

5. eShopSync 

eShopSync by Webkul is a two-way connector designed to link your BigCommerce storefront with your Salesforce CRM. It provides seamless, real-time data synchronization of orders, products, and customer details, ensuring that both your ecommerce platform and your CRM are connected. 

Focus: Provides a two-way sync between BigCommerce and Salesforce, streamlining operations and improving data accuracy for ecommerce businesses.

Integration potential: eShopSync offers deep integration with Agentforce Sales, Service, Marketing. It allows BigCommerce order and customer data to be immediately accessible and actionable within Salesforce, which is crucial for dropshipping businesses looking to provide high-quality, data-driven customer experiences.

Cost: eShopSync offers various pricing plans, including subscription options for different tiers of data synchronization volume and features. You can find detailed, up-to-date pricing information and trial offers on their AppExchange listing.

Tips to scale business beyond dropshipping suppliers 

Dropshipping is often a launchpad, not the final destination. To truly scale, SMBs must integrate their commerce operations with their sales, marketing, and service teams using a connected platform. Here’s how you can make your dropshipping data work harder for you.

Connect products with teams

Use the purchase data collected from your dropshipping suppliers for small business and stored in your CRM to segment your audience and send highly targeted marketing messages. 

As your business grows, your sales team can use customer data and product trends to identify upsell and cross-sell opportunities, driving higher average order values. 

Automate with AI

AI agents can help handle routine tasks‌ — ‌like notifying customers of shipping delays or updating inventory records‌ — ‌by acting as an intelligent partner that works across your sales, service, and commerce applications. This is a powerful application of digital labor.

Connect dropshipping with your CRM for SMB success

Dropshipping removes one of the biggest hurdles to starting an online business: managing inventory and shipping. By choosing one of the vetted suppliers or platforms we’ve reviewed, you’re setting a strong foundation for your small business. But the real power comes from connecting these operations.

The best dropshipping strategy is one where your commerce, service, and sales data work together. When you bring your supplier relationships, customer history, and sales insights into a unified platform like Salesforce, you move beyond just fulfilling orders. You move into lasting growth.

Start your journey with the Free or Starter Suite today. Looking for more customization? Explore Pro Suite. Already a Salesforce customer? Activate Foundations to try out Agentforce 360 today.

AI supported the writers and editors who created this article.

What is the most important factor when selecting dropshipping suppliers for small business? 
Reliability is the single most critical factor, encompassing product quality, inventory accuracy, and consistent, fast fulfillment times. Without reliability, your brand reputation is at risk.

How can a CRM help me manage my dropshipping business? 
A CRM centralizes all customer and supplier interaction data, allowing you to track order status, manage service tickets related to shipping, and use purchase history to inform marketing efforts, all from one platform.

Should I use multiple dropshipping suppliers? 
Yes, relying on a single supplier can be risky. Using multiple dropshipping suppliers for small business provides redundancy in case of stockouts or quality issues with one partner, protecting your fulfillment capabilities.

What is the difference between dropshipping and wholesale? 
Dropshipping means the supplier ships the product directly to your customer; you never hold inventory. Wholesale means you purchase products in bulk at a discount and store the inventory yourself before selling and shipping it.

How does Agentforce 360 integrate with a dropshipping model? 
Agentforce 360 acts as a smart operating layer that automates routine tasks between your commerce platform, CRM, and suppliers—for instance, automatically creating a service ticket when a shipment tracking update is late.
